title: To the Top
lines: On this morning in 1953, two men stood where no one ever had: the top of the world.
They didn't get there in a day. They got there by refusing to stop.
Whatever you're carrying, keep walking. The summit doesn't come to you.

title: Iron Sharpens Iron
lines: As iron sharpens iron, so one man sharpens another. — Proverbs 27:17
Nobody summits alone. Find your rope team and hold the line.
more: line: Type 1+2+1 for the climber's psalm.

title: I Lift My Eyes
lines: I lift up my eyes to the hills. From where does my help come?
My help comes from the LORD, the Maker of heaven and earth. — Psalm 121:1–2
more: line: Type 14×58 for the long haul.

title: Fifty-Eight Peaks
lines: Colorado holds 58 peaks over fourteen thousand feet. Nobody climbs them all in a weekend.
Money grows the same way: one steady climb at a time, and the view keeps getting better.

title: A little secret
lines: 42: the answer to life, the universe, and everything.

title: Well spotted
lines: 144: a dozen dozens, stacked like cordwood.
